message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "two different component types do not form a block (2 runes + 1 moonstone → 80 G)"
Current error: Expected { premium: 80 }, received { premium: NaN }
Passing tests so far: 9

Test calls quote with `[{type:"rune"}, {type:"rune"}, {type:"moonstone"}]` expecting `{premium: 80}` (= 2×25 + 1×25 + 5).

Spec says components — runes and moonstones — are each insured at 250 G with base premium 25 G. The rune-only block-of-3 logic must still work but should not match here (different types).

Add `moonstone: 25` to BASE_PREMIUM_BY_TYPE. The existing isThreeRuneBlock check needs to fail because not all 3 items are runes (one is moonstone), so the regular sum path applies: 25+25+25+5 = 80.

However, the prompt says spec rule: 3 alike (same-type) components form a block — generalize. But minimal step is: add moonstone:25 and update isThreeRuneBlock to require all items of same type AND that type is a component (rune/moonstone). For now just adding moonstone:25 may be enough since current check requires `every item.type === "rune"`, which 2 runes + 1 moonstone fails.

So: just add `moonstone: 25` to BASE_PREMIUM_BY_TYPE.

Do NOT refactor. EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal change to make the failing test pass.
